X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

ArubaGwClusterIntent_GwClusterIntentConf.description:
Gateway Cluster Intent Service (GCIS) enables policy-driven orchestration of
gateway clusters across organizational scopes such as global and site levels.
Unlike manual cluster configuration, GCIS automatically forms clusters based on
scope hierarchy and cluster-mode settings. This API can be used to create, modify,
or delete cluster intent profiles with configurations for cluster-mode, multicast
VLAN, heartbeat thresholds, IPv6 enablement, and CoA-VRRP settings.

profile.description: List of GW Cluster Intent Profiles.

cluster-mode.description: Cluster-Mode decides how the cluster profiles are formed automatically. Setting cluster-mode type to CM_MANUAL will disable the auto-formation of the cluster profiles.

x-enumDescriptions.CM_SITE: Auto-clustering is formed at Site level.

cluster-mode.x-typeDescription: Type of cluster-modes to set at DC, SC and Site scopes.

id.description: Specifies the starting VRRP ID for cluster members.

passphrase.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

vlan.description: VRRP VLAN corresponding to the VIP. Used for COA operations.
vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

default-gateway-mode.description:
Enable/disable one-to-one-redundancy mode for auto cluster profiles.
With this knob enabled, only 2 gateways are allowed in the cluster profile.
Leader is active and non-leader is standby. Required for BRANCH_GW.

device-type.description:
Persona of the GW cluster profile. Device-type of the GW cluster profile
should be one of the BRANCH_GW, MOBILITY_GW, VPNC.

x-enumDescriptions.ACCESS_SWITCH: Access Switch. This persona provides Access Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.AGG_SWITCH: Aggregation Switch. This persona provides Aggregation Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.ALL: ALL. This persona provides ALL devices functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.AOSS_ACCESS_SWITCH: AOS-S Access Switch. This persona provides Access Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.AOSS_AGG_SWITCH: AOS-S Aggregation Switch. This persona provides Aggregation Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.AOSS_CORE_SWITCH: AOS-S Core Switch. This persona provides Core Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.BRANCH_GW: Branch gateway. This gateway persona provides Aruba InstantOS and SD-Branch (LAN + WAN) functionality
x-enumDescriptions.BRIDGE: Bridge . This persona provides bridge functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.CAMPUS_AP: Campus/Branch AP. This AP persona provides WLAN functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.CORE_SWITCH: Core Switch. This persona provides Core Switch functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.HYBRID_NAC: Hybrid NAC Edge VM. This persona provides Hybrid NAC Edge VM functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.IOT: IoT Edge VM. This persona provides IoT Edge VM functionality.
x-enumDescriptions.MICROBRANCH_AP: Microbranch AP. This AP persona provides SD-WAN-lite functionality in addition to the functions of WLAN AP.
x-enumDescriptions.MOBILITY_GW: Mobility Gateway. This gateway persona provides Overlay WLAN and (or) wired LAN functionalities.
x-enumDescriptions.SERVICE_PERSONA: Service Persona. This persona provides service functionality.

ipv6-enable.description: Enable or disable the ipv6 mode in auto cluster profiles

multicast-vlan.description: VLAN to be used for forwarding multicast traffic to upstream router.
multicast-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

uplink-sharing.description:
Enable/disable uplink sharing for auto cluster profiles.
Applicable only for BRANCH_GW device-type with default-gateway-mode enabled.

uplink-tracking.description:
Enable/disable uplink tracking for auto cluster profiles.
With this knob enabled, Cluster leader will be selected based on number of active uplinks.
Applicable only for BRANCH_GW device-type with default-gateway-mode enabled.

cluster-mode.description: Cluster-Mode decides how the cluster profiles are formed automatically. Setting cluster-mode type to CM_MANUAL will disable the auto-formation of the cluster profiles.

x-enumDescriptions.CM_SITE: Auto-clustering is formed at Site level.

cluster-mode.x-typeDescription: Type of cluster-modes to set at DC, SC and Site scopes.

id.description: Specifies the starting VRRP ID for cluster members.

customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

get.description:
Gateway Cluster Intent Service (GCIS) enables policy-driven orchestration of
gateway clusters across organizational scopes such as global and site levels.
Unlike manual cluster configuration, GCIS automatically forms clusters based on
scope hierarchy and cluster-mode settings. This API can be used to create, modify,
or delete cluster intent profiles with configurations for cluster-mode, multicast
VLAN, heartbeat thresholds, IPv6 enablement, and CoA-VRRP settings.
get.summary: Read the configured Gateway Cluster Intent Service configuration

delete.description: List of GW Cluster Intent Profiles.
delete.summary: Delete a Gateway Cluster Intent profile

get.description: List of GW Cluster Intent Profiles.
get.summary: Read an existing Gateway Cluster Intent profile configuration

patch.description: List of GW Cluster Intent Profiles.
patch.summary: Update an existing Gateway Cluster Intent profile configuration

post.summary: Create a Gateway Cluster Intent profile
